name: James Brown
abstract: James Joseph Brown (May 3, 1933 – December 25, 2006) was an American singer, songwriter, dancer, musician, and record producer. The central progenitor of funk music and a major figure of 20th-century music, he is referred to by various nicknames, among them "Mr.

works_created.t: It's a Man's Man's Man's World

about_quotes.speaker: Doug Carn
about_quotes.evidence: So the girl said, “James Brown.” ... So I figured out what it was they wanted you know. So the director says, “Look, Doug, I could lose my job you know — I’m going out on a limb bringing a black group here and y’all are playing the God damn Beatles? Now you go back out there and play some of your mu
about_quotes.doc_title: Doug Carn interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 24, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Larry Gold
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses Fred Wesley's comments about writing for James Brown, but does not mention any personal interaction or relationship with James Brown himself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Larry Gold interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 18, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Porrello
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER mentions a 'James Brown thing' chart; INTERVIEWEE responds by scatting, showing recognition of the style/tune but no personal interaction is described.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Porrello interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 6, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Brecker
about_quotes.evidence: I had listened to him as a kid... I heard James Brown... for the first time and that had a — you know I probably sat up straight in bed. That just hit a nerve.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Brecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 12, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: I said, 'How can I put Coltrane together with James Brown?' ... He wrote, goddamn, Mother Popcorn and I Feel Good for James Brown, for God's sake.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Oliver Nelson Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: Because they have stuff from James Brown, they had stuff from Elvis Presley, they had jazz guys, they had a lot of, you know ownership of tunes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Oliver Nelson Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 10, 2020
